content.full_text:
August 12, 1988


Gretchen D. Franz
76-757 California Drive
Palm Desert, CA 92260

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-302

Dear Ms. Franz:

You have requested advice concerning your campaign disclosure
obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/

QUESTION

What are the campaign disclosure requirements for a county
central committee member who does not have a controlled committee
but who makes contributions from personal funds to other
California state and local government candidates?

CONCLUSION

County central committee members must file semiannual campaign
disclosure statements during any six-month period in which they
make or receive any contribution or make any expenditure.

FACTS

You are an elected member of the Riverside County Republican
Central Committee. You do not raise or spend any money on behalf
of your own candidacy. During the first six-months of 1988, you
made contributions from personal funds of less than $1,000 to
California state and local candidates and officeholders.


_____________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Gretchen D. Franz
Page 2, 1988

ANALYSIS

Elected officers and candidates are required to file
semiannual campaign disclosure statements. (Section 84200(a).)
Members of county central committees are elected officers under
the Act and must file semiannual campaign disclosure statements
during any six-month period in which they make or receive any
contribution or make any expenditure.2/ (Section 82023,
84200(a)(2).)

When you make or receive contributions totaling less than
$1,000 during the first six months of the year, and you do not
expect to exceed the $1,000 limit during the entire calendar year,
you may file a Form 470 on or before the due date for the last
semiannual statement due for that year (August 1 for 1988). A
properly completed Form 470 would fulfill your filing obligation
for the entire calendar year unless you exceed the $1,000 limit
noted above. If your reportable contributions total more than
$1,000 in a calendar year, you will be required to use Form 490.

In your letter, you list contributions which you made during
the first six-month period of 1988. Some of the contributions
were made to officeholders or candidates for federal office.
However, only those contributions you listed which were made to
candidates for or incumbents of California state or local
government office or to committees which are active at the state
or local level are reportable under the Act.

Enclosed are Forms 470 and 490. You may use whichever form is
appropriate for your circumstances. You should file an original
and one copy with your county clerk. Because the deadline for
filing your campaign statement was August 1, you may want to
request a waiver of the fine for late filing.

If you have further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

Bruce W Robeck
By: Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

_________________________

2/This analysis assumes that you do not receive a salary of
$100 or more per month as compensation for your elective office.
If this assumption is incorrect, please contact me for
clarification of your filing requirements.

July 22, 1988


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

After reading, and rereading several times, the
campaign material sent to me by the Riverside
County Elections Department (all 140 pages), neither
my husband nor I have been able to figure out
where or not I am supposed to fill out a campaign
disclosure statement. And, if I am, where is the
proper form?

I am a member, elected, of the Riverside County
Republican Central Committee. Have never spent
or collected any money on behalf of my campaign.
However, I have contributed to other campaigns or
committees in excess of $1,000.

For this reason, attached is a list of my
contributions.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]

Gretchen D. Franz
76-757 California Drive
Palm Desert, CA 92260
Riverside County
4th Supervisorial District

July 22, 1988


GRETCHEN D. FRANZ                    RIVERSIDE COUNTY REPUBLICAN
                                     CENTRAL COMMITTEE  4TH DISTRICT

76-757 California Drive, Palm Desert, CA 92260
(619) 345-3561

Retired

_________________________________________________________________

2/27/88    Committee to elect Myron Cole              $100.00
           Rancho Mirage City Council

3/16/88    George Bush campaign                       250.00

3/24/88    Bill Bradley 76 Club, State Assembly      100.00

4/25/88    Re-elect Al McCandless for Congress       250.00

5/27/88    California Republican Party                150.00
           Reception in Riverside
                                                      $850.00

•

Total amount of contributions under $100            $474.00

                                                    $1,324.00




[illegible]

central committee member who does not have a controlled committee
